| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile Android preview APK profile and EAS install compatibility | `apps/mobile/eas.json`, `apps/mobile/package.json`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Local configuration PASS, EAS remote APK build pending       | `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run check:eas` PASS; `corepack pnpm --version` from `apps/mobile` now reports `10.0.0`; `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` from `apps/mobile` PASS; `corepack pnpm run check:package-manager-scripts` PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check` PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck` PASS; preview env `expo config --json` confirmed Android package `com.eurekaworld.salaryhijacking`, owner `jinbizmans-organization`, staging API base URL, and configured Google services file path without printing secret values. | The first EAS preview builds failed during Install dependencies because `apps/mobile/package.json` pinned `pnpm@9.15.9` while the repository root requires `pnpm >=10 <11`; the mobile package manager and engine declarations now match the root. The preview profile remains `distribution: internal` and `android.buildType: apk`; the production profile remains `distribution: store` and `android.buildType: app-bundle`. Existing EAS project/owner were injected through the local PowerShell session only and were not committed. No Google Play submit, production AAB build, keystore reset, FCM reset, secret output, or Firebase JSON output was performed. Latest EAS remote build was created after the fix and was still in queue at the last check, so no APK install link had been generated yet. |
| EAS preview pnpm version selection                               | `apps/mobile/eas.json`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`                             | Local profile config PASS, next EAS preview rebuild required | EAS build `ea00ecf1-106a-4181-8cf9-9f82ae391204` failed during `INSTALL_DEPENDENCIES` with `ERR_PNPM_UNSUPPORTED_ENGINE`, `Expected version: >=10.0.0 <11`, and `Got: 9.15.5`. EAS build `3dcac8b9-b724-489a-b801-962451dc3e5a` proved that a package script hook alone is too late because EAS runs the hook through pnpm 9 first. GREEN local verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run check:eas` PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck` PASS; `corepack pnpm run check:package-manager-scripts` PASS.                                                                                | The final fix moves pnpm selection to the EAS `preview` build profile by setting `corepack: true` and `pnpm: "10.0.0"`, matching Expo's `eas.json` build schema. The temporary `eas-build-pre-install` hook was removed because it cannot run before pnpm engine validation when EAS starts from pnpm 9. Production remains `distribution: store` and `android.buildType: app-bundle`; no Google Play submit, production AAB build, keystore reset, FCM reset, secret output, or Firebase JSON output was performed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

## 2026-07-07 Cloudflare Worker Production Deploy Recovery

| Area                                                    | Path                                                                                                                                                                            | Status                                 | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cloudflare Worker connected deploy and runtime evidence | `release/cloudflare-runtime-evidence.json`, Cloudflare Workers `salary-hijacking-api`, `salary-hijacking-notifications`, `salary-hijacking-scheduler`, `salary-hijacking-admin` | Verified PASS, release readiness READY | Cloudflare connected builds for notifications build `e40d75b3-ae8d-42ce-9667-44418407989d` and scheduler build `a0a40c52-b32c-4475-ab07-b3f896bd05cd` both stopped with `build_outcome=success`; logs include `Uploaded ...`, `Deployed ... triggers`, and `Current Version ID` without `wrangler: not found`, Worker-name mismatch, or Queue consumer errors. Queue consumers now map production operations to `salary-hijacking-api`, notification retry/operations to `salary-hijacking-notifications`, and scheduler operations to `salary-hijacking-scheduler`. `release/cloudflare-observation.local.json` was regenerated with no secret values; `corepack pnpm run release:cloudflare-proof`; `corepack pnpm run release:cloudflare-evidence`; `corepack pnpm run check:release-readiness -- --soft` PASS/READY. | The failed deployment log was caused by stale Queue consumers created under `salary-hijacking-api` for notification/scheduler queues after an earlier aggregate deploy. Only the incorrect consumer records were removed; queues and messages were not deleted. Cloudflare certificate-pack API access returned 9109, so TLS proof was corroborated by successful `curl.exe -I` HTTPS checks for all six target domains without storing response bodies, headers, secrets, or certificate material. |

| Mobile Metro dependency and Android entry resolution | `apps/mobile/metro.config.cjs`, `apps/mobile/src/config/__tests__/metro-config.test.ts` | Verified PASS locally, CI rerun pending | CI log from GitHub Actions runs `28835219599` and `28835219593` showed `@salary-hijacking/mobile#test` failing only in `src/config/__tests__/metro-config.test.ts` because Windows subst path expectations were evaluated on the Linux runner. GREEN local verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- metro-config.test.ts --runInBand` PASS with 126 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test` PASS with 36 suites and 619 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck` PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check` PASS. | Metro path mapping now uses `path.win32` when explicitly validating Windows/subst paths, avoids PC-specific hard-coded workspace-root expectations in the test matrix, and adds a Linux-CI loaded regression case for `Y:\.` Android entry fallback. This is a CI quality gate fix, not native E2E proof; Android/iOS native build and EAS evidence remain release blockers. |

## 2026-07-07 Cloudflare Worker Secret Binding Evidence Contract

| Area                                                    | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Status                               | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cloudflare runtime Worker secret binding proof contract | `release/release-targets.json`, `release/cloudflare-runtime-evidence.json`, `release/examples/cloudflare-observation.local.example.json`, `release/README.md`, `scripts/release/collect-cloudflare-proof.mjs`, `scripts/release/collect-cloudflare-proof.test.mjs`, `scripts/release/generate-cloudflare-runtime-evidence.mjs`, `scripts/release/generate-cloudflare-runtime-evidence.test.mjs`, `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.mjs`, `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs` | Verified PASS, release still BLOCKED | RED `node --test scripts\release\collect-cloudflare-proof.test.mjs` failed because Worker secret binding proof accepted only a coarse boolean and did not read expected Worker secret names from the release target manifest; RED `node --test scripts\release\generate-cloudflare-runtime-evidence.test.mjs` failed because tracked Cloudflare runtime evidence did not preserve Worker secret names or missing names; RED `node --test scripts\release\check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "Worker secret bindings"` failed because readiness accepted `workerSecretBindingsVerified=true` without name-level proof. GREEN `node --test scripts\release\collect-cloudflare-proof.test.mjs scripts\release\generate-cloudflare-runtime-evidence.test.mjs scripts\release\check-release-readiness.test.mjs`; `corepack pnpm run check:release-readiness -- --soft`; `corepack pnpm run format:check`; `corepack pnpm run check:external-integrations`; `corepack pnpm run test:root-scripts` PASS with 253 root-script tests. | Cloudflare release targets now define the expected secret names for each Salary Hijacking Worker. The proof collector and evidence generator store only Worker secret names and missing-name lists, reject raw Worker secret payload keys such as `text` or `value`, and keep values out of tracked evidence. Release readiness now blocks the existing `cloudflare-runtime:worker-secret-bindings` gate unless the boolean proof is backed by complete Worker secret name coverage. Current readiness remains `198 PASS / 6 BLOCKED / 3 WARN = 95.7%` because the required Worker secret binding names, `DATABASE_URL`, `STAGING_DATABASE_URL`, `EXPO_TOKEN`, and `EAS_PROJECT_ID` still require trusted no-value proof. |

## 2026-07-05 Community Parser Sensitive Text Guard

| Area                                  | Path                                                                                                                                                                                             | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile community response text safety | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.parsers.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.redaction.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.parsers.test.ts` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- community.parsers.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "sensitive raw text"` failed because server community title/content/comment/author text with raw salary, email, or phone values could enter mobile UI models when explicit exposure flags were missing; GREEN same command PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- community.parsers.test.ts --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- community.redaction.test.ts community.service.test.ts community.integration.test.ts --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check` | Community post, detail, comment, author display, and tag parsing now fail closed when raw financial amount, email, phone, token, or similar sensitive text appears in server responses, even if the server forgot to set exposure flags. This protects community UI surfaces from rendering sensitive raw payloads while keeping normal anonymous, like, bookmark, attachment, and moderation parsing intact. |

| Mobile public app config error handling | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts public app config network failures"` failed with raw fetch error exposure; GREEN same command PASS; RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts malformed public app config JSON"` failed with raw JSON parser exposure; GREEN same command PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check` | Public `/api/v1/public/app-config` fetch failures are now converted to `PUBLIC_APP_CONFIG_NETWORK_ERROR`, and malformed JSON responses are converted to `PUBLIC_APP_CONFIG_INVALID_RESPONSE` before reaching UI flows, preventing raw request URLs, token-like network messages, parser messages, or copied response text from surfacing in the app. This is an app-code hardening slice, not production URL reachability proof. |

The collector rejects EAS tokens, Apple/Google credentials, binary artifact URLs or paths, signing keys, service account JSON, reviewer passwords, copied store-console payloads, logs, and non-AAB Android production build claims. Current tracked evidence intentionally remains unverified until real EAS build, native E2E/device-farm, Google Play dry-run, iOS build, and App Store dry-run proof is collected. |

The generated APK remains an ignored local E2E artifact, not a production store build. |

The current PC now detects local `adb` and `emulator`, but release readiness does not treat tool availability as native E2E proof. `nativeE2eVerified` remains false until Detox or equivalent device-farm evidence is recorded without secrets. The direct Detox preflight currently fails only because the local Android E2E APK is missing.                      |
| Android E2E APK import helper           | `apps/mobile/scripts/import-e2e-apk.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/import-e2e-apk.test.mjs`, `apps/mobile/package.json`, `package.json`, `release/README.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Verified PASS              | RED/GREEN `node --test apps\mobile\scripts\import-e2e-apk.test.mjs`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`; `corepack pnpm run quality`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Added a local-only helper that copies a verified `.apk` with an APK/ZIP header into `apps/mobile/build/e2e/android/salary-hijacking-e2e.apk`, the Detox binary path ignored by Git. The helper rejects artifact URLs, non-APK files, and placeholder text so EAS artifact links or credentials are not written into tracked files. The CLI success output now uses `process.stdout.write` instead of `console.log` to satisfy the mobile no-console lint rule. |
| Android Detox preflight next commands   | `apps/mobile/scripts/check-detox-env.mjs`, `apps/mobile/src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts`, `release/README.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Verified PASS, E2E BLOCKED | RED/GREEN `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile test -- mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts`; `node scripts\check-detox-env.mjs android.emu.debug` from `apps/mobile`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | The Android Detox preflight still exits 2 when the E2E APK is missing, but now prints the exact safe next commands: `build:e2e:android`, `e2e:android:import-apk -- <local-apk-path>`, and `test:e2e:android`. This adds the release evidence path but does not prove production secret configuration until a trusted environment runs it. |

## 2026-07-01 Git Remote HEAD Release Guard Update

| Area                          | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Git remote HEAD release guard | `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.mjs`, `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/codex/13_BASELINE_VERIFICATION_MAP.md`, `docs/codex/14_EXTERNAL_RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md` | Verified PASS | RED `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local release commit"`; GREEN `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local release commit"`; RED/GREEN `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local origin main tracking"`; `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs`; `corepack pnpm run test:root-scripts`; `corepack pnpm run check:external-integrations`; `corepack pnpm run check:release-readiness -- --soft`; `corepack pnpm run format:check` | Added a release-readiness guard that runs when authenticated Git push proof is claimed: it compares `git rev-parse HEAD` with `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` and blocks release readiness if the local release commit has not been pushed. When live remote reads are unavailable inside the local Node sandbox, it falls back to local `refs/remotes/origin/main` with a warning. This keeps the tracked external proof commit historical while preventing a stale local HEAD from being reported as release-ready. |

Neon still has the `salary-hijacking` project with ready `main` and `staging` branches plus read-write compute presence for both branches. No Neon host, connection string, password, token, or raw database URL is stored. Runtime secrets, Cloudflare deployed resources, DB migration/seed/smoke/rollback proof, public URL proof, and mobile native build/E2E/store-submit proof remain blockers. |

This does not deploy Workers, create Cloudflare resources, configure secrets, prove DNS/TLS/R2/Queue existence, or mark tracked `release/cloudflare-runtime-evidence.json` verified; it only creates a safer future CI proof path. |

Release readiness remains BLOCKED by runtime/external/native gates, not by storage. |

| Feature API bearer token attachment | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed while `createMobileAuthenticatedFetcher` was missing and feature API requests had no `Authorization` header; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 2 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck:strict`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 121 tests                                                                             | Shared mobile API factories now wrap budget, payroll, plan commitments, notifications, growth, profile, and community fetchers with `createMobileAuthenticatedFetcher()`. The wrapper reads the stored access token via `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY`, attaches only a valid bearer token, drops malformed token values, and keeps refresh tokens out of feature API request headers. Auth login/register remain unauthenticated entrypoints. Deployed API auth, DB user persistence, native E2E, and production smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                     |
| Auth refresh/logout session flow    | `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/types.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/constants.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                               | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand` failed while `api.refresh` and `api.logout` were missing; second RED failed because rejected refresh did not clear the local access token; GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 1 suite and 7 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck:strict`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 124 tests                                                     | Added `AuthApiClient.refresh()` for `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` and `AuthApiClient.logout()` for `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`. Refresh relies on the server HttpOnly refresh cookie with `credentials: include`, stores only the rotated access token, never stores raw refresh tokens, and clears the local access token on rejected refresh. Logout calls server session revocation/cookie clearing and deletes only the local access token. Deployed API smoke, DB staging proof, native E2E, and store-console proof remain separate release gates.                                                                                 |
| MY logout server wiring             | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/clean-fintech-screens.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/profile/index.tsx`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                      | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because `createMobileAuthApi()` ignored the injected token store; RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand` failed because MY had no `profileAuthApi.logout`/`logoutSession` wiring; GREEN targeted reruns passed 1 suite/3 tests and 1 suite/21 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 125 tests                                                                    | `createMobileAuthApi()` now uses an injected auth-capable token store when supplied and keeps SecureStore as the runtime default. The Clean Fintech MY screen now exposes a logout action that calls `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` through `AuthApiClient.logout()`, clears the local profile snapshot after server success, routes back to `/(auth)/login`, and keeps raw refresh tokens out of app storage. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                            |
| Logout local token cleanup          | `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/05_PRIVACY_ADS_SECURITY.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because rejected server logout left the local access token undeleted; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 8 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 129 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS                                                                                                                          | `AuthApiClient.logout()` now attempts local `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY` deletion in a `finally` block so the app does not keep trusting a local bearer token after the user asks to log out, even when the server revoke request returns an error. The server logout request, HttpOnly cookie boundary, privacy headers, and no-refresh-token-storage rule remain intact. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Feature API 401 refresh retry       | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                             | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because a protected feature API 401 immediately surfaced as login-required without calling refresh; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 4 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 126 tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Shared feature API fetchers now perform one `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` attempt through the raw Auth API when a protected API returns 401, rely on the server HttpOnly refresh cookie, store only the rotated access token, and retry the original feature request once with the new bearer token. Failed refresh returns the original 401 to the feature parser to avoid loops or false success. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                     |
| Feature API refresh single-flight   | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because two concurrent protected API 401 responses triggered two refresh calls; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 5 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 127 tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Shared authenticated fetchers now keep one in-flight refresh promise per fetcher instance. Concurrent protected API 401 responses await the same refresh attempt, then retry their original requests individually, reducing duplicated refresh calls during startup or multi-tab hydration while preserving the one-retry/no-false-success behavior. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Root bootstrap auth refresh retry   | `apps/mobile/app/_layout.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/app-screen-contract.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- app-screen-contract.test.ts` failed because root bootstrap did not reference `/api/v1/auth/refresh`; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 4 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 128 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run export:web`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run build`: PASS, 12 tasks | Root `/api/v1/mobile/bootstrap` now retries once after a 401 by using the existing Auth API refresh client, the server HttpOnly refresh cookie, and SecureStore access-token storage through `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY`. Privacy headers, platform headers, correlation IDs, server-authoritative bootstrap behavior, and offline fallback remain intact. Native E2E/store proof, deployed API/DB smoke, runtime secrets, Cloudflare runtime evidence, and public URL proof remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                             |

The mobile client now calls `POST /api/v1/users/me/support-tickets` with privacy-safe flags, validates subject/message length and sensitive-keyword exclusions, and rejects unsafe support responses. The API route accepts privacy-safe support tickets through the users route in-memory fallback, emits an audit-compatible user event, returns only ticket id/status/category/subject plus safety flags, and does not echo `userId`, message body, raw financial/personal data, raw push tokens, or ad-targeting payloads. DB persistence for support tickets, deployed API smoke, native E2E, store proof, Cloudflare runtime proof, and staging DB smoke remain separate release gates. |

## 2026-07-03 Mobile No-EAS Android Debug Build Path

| Area                                              | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Status           | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Expo-auth-free local Android debug APK build path | `apps/mobile/package.json`, `package.json`, `apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.test.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-detox-env.mjs`, `apps/mobile/src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts`, `scripts/dev/clean-generated-junk.mjs`, `scripts/dev/clean-generated-junk.test.mjs`, `scripts/quality/check-external-integrations.mjs`, `scripts/quality/check-external-integrations.test.mjs`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified partial | RED actual build attempts exposed sequential native blockers: missing Android SDK `local.properties`, stale `expo.core.ExpoModulesPackage` autolinking, missing `androidx.core:core-splashscreen`, missing SecureStore backup XML resources, Expo wrapper classpath gaps, and Windows CMake path instability. GREEN checks: `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run build:e2e:android:local-debug:preflight`: PASS; `node --test apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.test.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/eas-local-android-build.test.mjs scripts/dev/clean-generated-junk.test.mjs`: PASS, 13 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts --runInBand`: PASS, 4 tests; `corepack pnpm run check:package-manager-scripts`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run check:external-integrations`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS | Added a guarded no-EAS local Android debug build wrapper that checks workspace-local Expo CLI, Java/JBR, Android SDK tools, writes `android/local.properties`, injects required generated Android dependency/resource fixes, patches stale RN autolinking output, repairs Reanimated CMake directories, copies a verified APK header to `apps/mobile/build/e2e/android/salary-hijacking-e2e.apk`, and is exposed through mobile package scripts and Detox guidance. Actual APK generation is still blocked on this Windows/pnpm path by Expo native CMake `build.ninja` instability under the long real `.pnpm` package path; using `C:\shp` as a junction did not resolve it because Gradle still observed the real workspace path. This does not prove Android production AAB, native E2E, or store submission. Next safe routes are WSL/CI, a real short-path worktree/install, or EAS with verified `EXPO_TOKEN` evidence. |

## 2026-07-03 Mobile Android Store Permission Minimization

| Area                                  | Path                                                                                                                                | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Android store permission minimization | `apps/mobile/app.config.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts --runInBand`: FAIL because broad storage/overlay permissions were not blocked; GREEN same command: PASS, 1 suite and 5 tests. Broader verification is recorded in the final task report. | Added Expo `blockedPermissions` for `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, `WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, and `SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW` so regenerated native Android manifests remove broad storage/overlay access. This keeps the finance app permission surface focused on server-authoritative finance, notifications, biometric access, vibration, internet, and deep links. Production AAB, native E2E, Play Console proof, and runtime Cloudflare/secret evidence remain release gates. |

Fixed the Cloudflare proof collector CLI so it reads expected Worker/admin targets from `release/release-targets.json` instead of rejecting valid observed Worker names as unexpected. Remaining blockers: R2 must be enabled in Cloudflare Dashboard, Worker secret bindings need no-value proof from an approved secret store, `salaryhijacking.com` zone/custom domains/TLS need sufficient Cloudflare permissions and registrar/DNS setup, public URL proof is still missing, and runtime secrets/DB smoke/iOS/store-submit gates remain separate release blockers. |

If the server write fails, the mission completion state is removed and the toast tells the user that completion was not reflected. Offline fallback missions without a server task still complete locally as non-authoritative UI fallback. |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 59 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Notification archive and delete actions still give fast local feedback, but if the server mutation fails the app restores the removed notification and unread count before showing the failure message. This prevents the app from leaving a local-success state for an unpersisted notification mutation and keeps notification state aligned with the server as the authority. |

| Area                                                   | Path                                                                                                                                                                     | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 62 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Split server-authoritative expense creation from the optional receipt attach step. If the expense is created but receipt attachment fails, the app keeps the server-created expense, clears the stale receipt attachment, refreshes server budget/expense snapshots, and tells the user to attach the receipt again instead of adding a duplicate offline preview expense. |

| ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 63 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Split community post creation from optional attachment linking. If the post is created but uploaded attachments fail to link, the app clears the submitted draft/attachment queue, keeps the server-created post as the successful source of truth, and tells the user only the attachment connection failed instead of reporting the whole post creation as failed. |

| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 6 tests in the budget utils suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 33 suites and 200 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS. | Tightened `parseKrwInputAmount()` so mobile expense/budget inputs accept either plain digits or correctly grouped Korean currency commas only. Malformed comma grouping is rejected instead of being silently converted into a different KRW amount, preserving the platform rule that financial inputs must be explicit positive KRW integers. |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile Auth Access Token Storage Guard

| Area                                          | Path                                                                                                                                                  | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile auth access token storage guard        | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "malformed access tokens"` failed because a CR/LF-bearing access token was accepted and could be written to secure storage. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 12 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 33 suites and 201 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS. `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS. `git diff --check`: PASS. | Login and signup auth response normalization now validates server-issued access tokens before the mobile app can persist them. Tokens longer than 8192 characters or containing CR/LF/tab characters are rejected as invalid auth responses, so malformed bearer material is not written into the platform token store. Refresh tokens and delivery/reset secrets remain server/cookie-bound and are not persisted by the mobile app. |
| Mobile bearer-prefixed token storage guard    | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "bearer-prefixed"` failed because `Bearer server-token` was accepted and could be written to secure storage. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 13 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                                             | Access tokens with any whitespace are now rejected before mobile persistence. This prevents a server or proxy response that includes an Authorization header value such as `Bearer ...` from being stored as the raw token and later rewrapped into an invalid or unsafe bearer header.                                                                                                                                               |
| Mobile authorization header token guard       | `apps/mobile/src/shared/storage/auth-token.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/storage/__tests__/auth-token.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/storage/__tests__/auth-token.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "bearer-prefixed"` failed because a bearer-prefixed value already present in secure storage still produced an `authorization` header. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 3 tests in the auth token storage helper suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                 | Stored mobile access tokens are now rejected at request-header attachment time when they contain any whitespace. This defense-in-depth guard prevents stale or externally corrupted secure-storage values such as `Bearer access.jwt.token` from being sent as `Bearer Bearer access.jwt.token`, while preserving valid compact access tokens.                                                                                        |
| Mobile whitespace-padded token response guard | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "whitespace-padded"` failed because a server access token with leading/trailing spaces was silently trimmed and could be stored. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 14 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                         | Auth response normalization now validates access tokens from the raw server string instead of the generic trimmed text helper. This prevents the mobile app from silently rewriting malformed server-issued token material before persistence, while preserving trim behavior for non-token fields such as user identifiers and status text.                                                                                          |
| Mobile non-positive token TTL guard           | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "non-positive access token TTLs"` failed because `accessTokenExpiresIn: 0` was silently converted into a 1-second persisted session. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 15 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                     | Auth response normalization now rejects explicitly non-positive access token TTLs instead of clamping them to one second. This prevents login/refresh from persisting immediately expired or contract-invalid bearer tokens while keeping the default 900-second fallback for responses that omit TTL fields.                                                                                                                         |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile Community Raw JWT Guard

| Area                                     | Path                                                                                                                                                                             | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile community raw JWT guard           | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.redaction.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.validators.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/community/__tests__/community.validators.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "raw JWT exposure"` failed because a raw JWT-like token without a `token:` keyword could be posted. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 7 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.   | Community validation now treats bare JWT-shaped strings as sensitive personal/auth data. This prevents users from accidentally publishing raw bearer/session material even when the text does not include obvious words such as token, bearer, refresh, push, or fcm.                                                   |
| Mobile community raw JWT redaction guard | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.redaction.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.redaction.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`  | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/community/__tests__/community.redaction.test.ts --runInBand` failed because a raw JWT-like token remained in `redactCommunityText()` output. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 3 tests. Broader verification is recorded in the final task report.                                                     | Community redaction now masks bare JWT-shaped auth material with `[auth-redacted]` before applying the existing email, phone, resident ID, card, account, raw KRW, and keyword-token redactions. This keeps service/error/preview paths that use redaction from echoing raw bearer/session material back to the app UI. |
| Mobile community report reason redaction | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.service.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.service.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`      | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/community/__tests__/community.service.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts sensitive report reason"` failed because report reasons sent raw phone/account/JWT text to the API payload. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 4 tests. Broader verification is recorded in the final task report. | Community report payloads now redact sensitive user-entered reason text before calling `reportPost()` or `reportComment()`. This preserves the moderation workflow while preventing raw phone, account, token, JWT, KRW, or identifier strings from being copied into mobile API transport payloads.                    |
| Mobile upload sensitive file-name guard  | `apps/mobile/src/features/uploads/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/uploads/__tests__/uploads.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`                              | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/uploads/__tests__/uploads.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "blocks sensitive Korean"` failed because sensitive Korean/identifier/token-like file names reached `Request` creation as a generic TypeError instead of a domain upload error. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 5 tests.         | Direct community attachment and variable-expense receipt uploads now reject file names containing Korean finance/privacy keywords, phone/account-like identifiers, or JWT-shaped tokens before network access. This prevents raw sensitive labels from being copied into the `x-upload-file-name` header.               |

The mobile client returns only provider/state/challenge/redirect/authorization URL fields and intentionally drops server `codeVerifier` material instead of persisting or exposing PKCE secrets. `expo-web-browser` opens a provider session only when the server returns an HTTPS `authorizationUrl`; deployed provider credentials and callback completion remain separate release gates. |

RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "OAuth callback"` failed because `app/auth/oauth/callback.tsx` did not exist. The new callback route reads `code` and `state`, calls `AuthApiClient.completeOAuth()` against `POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/callback`, persists only the access token, clears the one-time verifier, and routes authenticated users to `/salary` while failed callbacks return to login. Provider production credentials and deployed callback smoke remain separate release gates. |

OAuth start now emits provider `authorizationUrl` values when a public provider client id is configured, including PKCE challenge parameters, without requiring or exposing provider client secrets. |

| Area                                | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

| Mobile/API app-generated OAuth PKCE | `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `services/api/src/routes/auth.routes.ts`, `services/api/tests/auth-routes-env-secret.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api test -- auth-routes-env-secret.test.ts --runInBand -t "mobile app scheme"` failed because the API ignored the mobile `codeChallenge` and returned a server-generated challenge/verifier. GREEN targeted reruns: `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api test -- auth-routes-env-secret.test.ts --runInBand -t "mobile app scheme"` PASS, 26 files and 66 API tests in the Vitest run; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "app-generated PKCE"` PASS, 17 tests. Broader verification in the task report includes full API/mobile tests, typecheck, format, diff, release readiness, and junk cleanup. | Mobile OAuth start now generates the PKCE verifier/challenge inside the app with `expo-crypto`, sends only the challenge to `GET /api/v1/auth/oauth`, stores only the local verifier under the returned state, and never depends on a verifier in the server response. The API accepts client-generated S256 challenges, stores a prefixed verification value for callback validation, omits `codeVerifier` from client-challenge start responses, and keeps the legacy server-generated verifier path compatible for older callers. |

The server returns enumeration-safe `accepted: true`, stores a fresh token only for existing users, emits a security event, and does not echo the raw email. The mobile client strips delivery-only tokens and stores no verification token. |
